rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Crane Co.S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss In Favor Of Pending Federal Action
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 8, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s joinder to Crane Co.¿s motion to stay or dismiss in favor of pending federal action is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the requirements of C.C.P. 410.30 and Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal. 3d 744 are met. Plaintiffs¿ Federal Court action has resolved so this motion is moot. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES MCPEEK VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 8, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s special demurrers to the fourteenth (brake shoe grinding machine-negligence) and fifteenth (brake shoe grinding machine-product liability) causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the power tools at issue in that case had no function until they were used with toxic-containing disks. (Id. at 584 (¿[U]se with the specified wheels, discs, and grinders was the inevitable use.¿). Also, in Tellez-Cordova, the allegations were that the wheels and discs were harmless without the power supplied by respondents¿ tools, while here the allegations are that Hennessy¿s machines were used on asbestos-containing brakes. It is the asbestos content of a product that makes it inherently dangerous. (Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 564.) Defendant¿s machines are more analogous to the stove in Garman. As in that case, it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in Hennessy¿s machines is that they caused another defective product to injure the decedent. The defendant has no duty under these facts. .....(Part one - to be continued)

rulings.Case Title: DOLYE D GREEN et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 3, Defendant Shell Oil Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether the pipe insulation to which Mr. Green was exposed by Shell¿s employees contained asbestos. Even if plaintiffs had created a triable issue on that topic, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Green¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant (pipe insulation) was a substantial factor in causing his mesothelioma pursuant to Texas law, including Borg-Warner v. Flores (Texas 2007) 232 S.W.3d 765; Georgia-Pacific Corp. v. Stephens (Tex. App. 2007) 239 S.W.3d 304; Georgia-Pacific Corp. v. Bostic (Tex. App. 2010) 320 S.W.3d 588; Smith v. Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc. (Tex. App. 2010) 307 S.W.3d 829. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Green¿s declaration, Mr. Ewing¿s declaration and Dr. Horn¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
